sorting-direction.label: Sorting Direction (ASC, DESC)
sorting-direction.summary: Control sort order with ASC and DESC keywords.

sk1.title: Sorting with ASC and DESC
sk1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, you can control the order of your query results using the `ORDER BY` clause. By default, results are sorted in ascending order (smallest to largest or A-Z). To specify the direction, use `ASC` for ascending or `DESC` for descending order.

**Example:**

To list all products by price from lowest to highest:
```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
```

To list products by price from highest to lowest:
```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
```

You can also sort by multiple columns, specifying the direction for each:
```sql
SELECT name, category, price FROM products ORDER BY category ASC, price DESC;
```
This sorts first by category (A-Z), then by price within each category (highest to lowest).

q1.title: Default Sorting Direction
q1.prompt: What is the default sorting direction when you use ORDER BY in SQL without specifying ASC or DESC?
q1.hint: Think about what happens if you just use ORDER BY column.

help.concept: ORDER BY sorts results in ascending order by default unless DESC is specified.
help.hint_1: Ascending order is the default in most SQL databases.
help.hint_2: If you don't specify, results go from smallest to largest or A-Z.

q2.title: Sorting Directions in Practice
q2.prompt: Which of the following queries will sort products from the highest to lowest price?
q2.hint: Look for queries that use DESC with the price column.

help.concept: DESC sorts values in descending order, from highest to lowest.
help.hint_1: Only queries with DESC on price will sort from highest to lowest.
help.hint_2: ASC means ascending, so avoid those for highest-to-lowest sorting.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC;
options.b: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price ASC;
options.c: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price;
options.d: SELECT * FROM products ORDER BY price DESC, name ASC;

q3.title: Order the Steps: Sorting by Multiple Columns
q3.prompt: Arrange the SQL keywords to correctly sort products by category (A-Z), then by price (highest to lowest).
q3.hint: Start with the correct answer, then list columns and directions.

help.concept: When sorting by multiple columns, specify each column and its direction, separated by commas.
help.hint_1: First comes the column for primary sorting, then its direction.
help.hint_2: Use a comma to separate columns and specify the correct answer for the correct answer.

q4.title: Fill in the Blank: Sorting Syntax
q4.prompt:
Complete the SQL statement to sort products by stock in descending order:

SELECT name, stock FROM products ORDER BY stock ____;
q4.hint: Which keyword means 'from highest to lowest'?

help.concept: the missing term is used to sort values in descending order.
help.hint_1: ASC is for ascending, the missing term is for descending.
help.hint_2: You want the products with the most stock first.

q4.template: SELECT name, stock FROM products ORDER BY stock ____;

q5.title: Write a Query: Sort by Name Descending
q5.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all columns from the products table, sorted by name in descending (Z-A) order.
q5.hint: Use ORDER BY with the name column and specify the correct direction.

help.concept: ORDER BY lets you sort results by a column; DESC sorts from Z to A for text.
help.hint_1: The column to sort by is name. Use DESC for descending order.
help.hint_2: Remember to select all columns and use the correct table name.
